summary: Found 4 new issues in PartnerTables: false empty-state rendering on load failures, table edits cannot clear an area assignment, shared code-settings updates can partially apply and leave data inconsistent, and desktop reordering is still inaccessible to keyboard users.

findings.title: Editing a table cannot remove its current area assignment
findings.description: The edit form maps "No area" to an empty string, but `saveTable()` only adds `payload.area` when `tableForm.area` is truthy. Updating an existing table from an area to "No area" therefore sends no `area` field at all, so the record keeps its previous area on the backend and in local state.
findings.suggested_fix: When saving an existing table, always send an explicit cleared value for the link, e.g. `area: tableForm.area || null`, and mirror that in the optimistic state update. If create requests should omit the field, branch create/edit behavior separately.

findings.title: Area/Table fetch failures are silently shown as an empty page
findings.description: `listFor()` swallows every non-429 error and returns `[]`. `reload()` then treats the page as successfully loaded, so a transient backend or network failure renders the normal empty-state UI and leaves create/edit actions enabled. Users can be misled into thinking all areas/tables are gone and create duplicates against data that still exists.
findings.suggested_fix: Propagate non-success fetch failures out of `listFor()` and drive `loadError` from them, or track partial-load failures explicitly so the page stays in an error state instead of masquerading as empty data.

findings.title: Shared code-settings save can leave partner mode and table codes out of sync
findings.description: In shared-code mode, the handler updates the Partner record and local partner state before rewriting table codes in batches. If any later table update fails, the catch only shows a generic toast; the partner may already be switched to shared mode while only some tables have new `code`/`verification_code` values. That creates inconsistent QR and verification behavior until someone repairs it manually.
findings.suggested_fix: Make the save atomic from the UI's perspective: update all tables first and flip partner settings last, or reload/rollback both partner and table state when any batch step fails. A bulk endpoint/transaction is the safest option.

findings.title: Desktop reordering is not keyboard-accessible
findings.description: `GripHandle` is focusable and exposed with button semantics, but it only supports `onDragStart` and has no keyboard interaction. The same handle is used for area reorder and desktop table reorder, while the move up/down buttons are hidden on `sm` and above. Keyboard users on desktop therefore cannot reorder areas or tables, and the announced "button" is a dead end.
findings.suggested_fix: Provide keyboard reorder controls on all breakpoints or add explicit keyboard semantics for the handle, and remove `role="button"`/`tabIndex` if the handle remains mouse-only.

missing_tests: Regression test that editing a table and selecting "No area" clears the persisted area link.
Failure-path test that Area/Table fetch errors surface `loadError` instead of rendering the empty-state UI.
Failure-path test for shared code-settings save where one table update fails mid-batch and the UI reloads or rolls back to a consistent state.
Accessibility coverage for keyboard-only reordering of areas/tables on desktop.
notes: The task referenced `pages/PartnerTables/base/partnertables.jsx`, but the workspace snapshot only exposed the latest implementation through the PartnerTables release artifact reviewed above.
No automated tests were run; this review is based on static inspection of the page snapshot plus `BUGS.md`/`README.md`.
